claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard enabling agents to connect to external tools with isolation: each tool runs in its own process
actionable: Use MCP to connect agents to external services; tools in separate processes prevent crashes from propagating to the agent
source_title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)

claim: MCP tools describe themselves with schemas at runtime, enabling agents to discover capabilities without hardcoded tool definitions
actionable: Build tools that expose their schema via MCP; agents can then use tools without manual integration code
source_title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)

claim: MCP tools can be written in any language (Python, Go, Node) and still be used by agents, enabling polyglot tool development
actionable: Write tools in the language best suited to each task; MCP abstracts language differences
source_title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)

claim: ADK agents automatically wrap Python functions as ADK tools with schema inference from docstrings and signatures
actionable: When building tools for ADK agents, use Python docstrings and type hints; ADK auto-generates schemas
source_title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
